PulmoScan
CNN pneumonia screening demo
Demo Metrics Architecture Results
Runs entirely in your browser — no server, no upload

A CNN that reads
chest X-rays for pneumonia.

A compact 3-block convolutional network (~102K parameters) trained to separate NORMAL from PNEUMONIA chest radiographs. The trained weights run client-side via a hand-written JavaScript inference engine — no TensorFlow.js, no backend.

NO IMAGE
LOADED
Samples are procedurally generated demo X-rays (see note below). Any image you upload is processed locally and never leaves your device.

Prediction

This image's texture doesn't match the model's synthetic training data. The verdict below is not trustworthy — treat it as noise, not a diagnosis.
Load a scan to begin
 
P(Normal)
—
P(Pneumonia)
—
Demo model, not a medical device. This network was trained on a procedurally generated synthetic dataset (see the Dataset note in Results) so the pipeline runs fully offline. It is a methodology demonstration, not a validated diagnostic tool.
Test-set performance

How the model scored

Held-out test set, synthetic demo dataset (200 images, class-balanced).

Model

Architecture

Three Conv–BatchNorm–ReLU–Pool blocks, Global Average Pooling, Dropout, and a small dense classifier head.

Input
64×64×1
→
Conv 32
64×64×32
→
Pool
32×32×32
→
Conv 64
32×32×64
→
Pool
16×16×64
→
Conv 128
16×16×128
→
Pool
8×8×128
→
GAP
128
→
Dense 64
ReLU
→
Dense 1
Sigmoid
Training run

Curves & diagnostics

Generated by train.py on the synthetic demo dataset.

Training / validation accuracy and loss across epochs
Confusion matrix, test set
ROC curve, test set
Dataset note: trained on a procedural synthetic proxy (Gaussian opacity patches simulating consolidation) so this demo runs fully offline. Swap in the real Kaggle “Chest X-Ray Images (Pneumonia)” dataset via the accompanying train.py --data_dir flag for real-world results.
Built with a hand-rolled JS CNN inference engine — no external ML runtime required.
